one_line_ask: Turn the client's five supplied concept rasters (one logo + four challenge-coin references) into clean, coin-ready vector marks, each delivered as transparent-PNG and SVG in black, white, gold, and silver.
brief:
A challenge-coin / branding client (automotive-industrial sector) supplies five flat reference images: one primary logo concept and four symbolic coin-face concepts. They are pixelated mock-ups that cannot go to a mint. The task is to convert each into production-grade vector art and deliver a four-color family per mark.

Pipeline per the connector chain: (1) upload all five client rasters to CC storage and preview them; (2) on the primary logo, remove the busy background so only the mark survives on transparency; (3) auto-straighten the de-bagged logo so the mark sits square (coins are minted on a true axis); (4) crop the straightened logo tight to its own bounds so the trace has no dead margin; (5) vectorize that clean, cropped, transparent logo into a scalable SVG — this is the editable master vector that also seeds EPS/PDF on packaging; (6) take the vector-master raster proof and select the mark's silhouette, then fill it with solid true-black to mint the BLACK variant; (7) on the same master, apply a flat color overlay in gold #C8A24B to mint the GOLD variant; (8) apply a monochromatic silver/gray tint to mint the SILVER variant (white #FFFFFF is the transparent-on-light case,
